purpose: Authenticate users; manage session lifecycle; lock accounts after repeated failed attempts.

entities.name: Session
entities.description: A logged-in instance of a User.

entities.name: Account
entities.description: Lockout state derived from failed attempts.

requirements.description: When a registered user submits valid credentials, the system shall create an Active session owned by that user.

ears.trigger: a registered user submits valid credentials
ears.response: create an Active session owned by that user

requirements.description: While a user's session is Active, when the user requests logout, the system shall transition that session to LoggedOut.
If a login attempt fails while the account is Unlocked, then the system shall increment failedAttempts and lock the account when it reaches MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS.

ears.response: increment failedAttempts and lock the account when it reaches MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS

requirements.description: While a session has been inactive for longer than MAX_SESSION_AGE, the system shall transition it to Expired.

ears.state: a session has been inactive for longer than MAX_SESSION_AGE

invariants.description: At most one Active session per user.
A Locked account has no Active session.
Expired/LoggedOut sessions never appear in any user's active set.

constraints.name: MAX_SESSION_AGE
constraints.description: Modeled as a step counter for bounded checking.

states.name: Active
states.description: Session is live and accepted by the server.

states.name: Locked
states.description: Locked due to repeated failed login attempts. Not terminal — recoverable via unlock_account.

decisions.title: JWT (RS256) over session cookies
decisions.context: Edge proxy needs to validate tokens without a round-trip to the auth service.
decisions.decision: Use JWT signed with RS256 for session tokens.
decisions.rationale: Asymmetric signing lets the edge verify with the public key; private key stays in the auth service.

alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Edge can't validate without a network hop.
Extra request per call adds latency and ops cost.

open_questions.question: Should unlock_account be available to a self-service flow (e.g. password reset) or only to Admin?
Session expiration: is it strictly time-based (inactivity timeout), or also bounded by absolute session age?
